title: salvia officinalis subsp. oxyodon
intro: Salvia officinalis subsp. oxyodon, also known as Red Sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia officinalis plant, a member of the mint family, characterized by its red-colored inflorescence and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Red Sage include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of consuming Red Sage include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Red Sage can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
